llrs.title: E2E header is prepended as first 10 bytes of the serialised payload
llrs.description: The `Protector::protect()` function shall produce an output byte slice whose first 10 bytes are the E2E header (DataID 2 B, SourceID 2 B, SeqCtr 4 B, CRC16 2 B) and whose remaining bytes are the original payload.
llrs.rationale: Ensures the downstream `Receiver` can locate the header at a fixed offset without out-of-band signalling.

llrs.title: CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE is computed over the first 8 header bytes (CRC slot = 0) concatenated with the payload
llrs.description: The CRC field (bytes 8–9 of the header) shall be computed using the CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE algorithm (init=0xFFFF, poly=0x1021, no reflection) with the CRC slot treated as 0x0000 during computation.
llrs.rationale: Matches the standard E2E Profile 2 / AUTOSAR-compatible wire format; allows the receiver to validate without knowing the sender's counter value in advance.

llrs.title: Sequence counter increments monotonically by 1 on each call to protect()
llrs.description: `Protector` shall maintain an `AtomicU32` sequence counter initialised to 0 and incremented by 1 (wrapping on overflow) on each call to `protect()`.
llrs.rationale: Provides the sequence integrity required for loss-of-frame detection at the receiver.

llrs.title: Receiver rejects payloads shorter than 10 bytes with HeaderTooShort
llrs.description: `Receiver::unwrap()` shall return `E2EErrorKind::HeaderTooShort` when the input slice length is less than `HEADER_SIZE` (10).
llrs.rationale: Prevents out-of-bounds slice indexing on malformed or truncated frames.
llrs.implementation: src/safety/mod.rs — `Receiver::unwrap()` length guard at top of function

llrs.title: Receiver rejects frames with CRC mismatch
llrs.description: `Receiver::unwrap()` shall recompute the CRC over the received header (CRC slot = 0) and payload; if the recomputed CRC differs from the header's CRC field the function shall return `E2EErrorKind::CrcMismatch`.
llrs.rationale: Detects random transmission bit errors (Hamming distance ≥ 4 up to 32767 bits for CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE).

llrs.title: Receiver rejects frames where the sequence counter is not consecutive
llrs.description: After the first successfully received frame, `Receiver::unwrap()` shall return `E2EErrorKind::SequenceGap` whenever the received counter is not equal to `last_seq.wrapping_add(1)`.
llrs.rationale: Detects dropped or reordered frames, satisfying the sequence integrity requirement.
llrs.implementation: src/safety/mod.rs — `Receiver::unwrap()` sequence gap check; `ReceiverState.first` flag for skip on first frame

llrs.title: Config supplies DataID and SourceID to header builder
llrs.description: `Config.data_id` (u16) and `Config.source_id` (u16) are written into bytes 0–1 and 2–3 of the E2E header as little-endian values on every call to `protect()`.
llrs.rationale: Allows the receiver to distinguish frames from different data elements and sources.

llrs.title: CRC is stored little-endian in header bytes 8–9
llrs.description: The 16-bit CRC value shall be written to bytes 8–9 of the header using `u16::to_le_bytes()`.
llrs.rationale: Consistent endianness ensures interoperability between any sender/receiver pair on the bus.

llrs.title: Sequence counter is stored little-endian in header bytes 4–7
llrs.description: The 32-bit sequence counter shall be written to bytes 4–7 of the header using `u32::to_le_bytes()`.
llrs.rationale: Fixed offset and endianness allows the receiver to extract the counter without configuration.

llrs.title: Sequence counter uses fetch_add with SeqCst ordering
llrs.description: `Protector::protect()` shall increment the sequence counter using `AtomicU32::fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst)` to ensure correct ordering under concurrent calls from multiple threads.
llrs.rationale: ISO 26262 ASIL-B requires safe concurrent access; SeqCst ordering prevents counter reuse due to reordering.

llrs.title: CRC check uses recomputed CRC, not a stored value
llrs.description: `Receiver::unwrap()` shall rebuild the expected header using the received DataID, SourceID, and sequence counter (with CRC slot = 0), then compute the CRC independently. It shall compare this freshly computed CRC against the header's CRC field — never cache or trust a previously computed CRC.
llrs.rationale: Ensures the CRC check cannot be bypassed by replaying a frame with a cached CRC.

llrs.title: first-frame flag prevents false SequenceGap on initial receive
llrs.description: `ReceiverState.first` shall be initialised `true` and cleared after the first successfully validated frame. The sequence gap check shall be skipped when `first == true`.
llrs.rationale: The receiver cannot know the sender's initial counter value; treating any first frame's counter as the baseline avoids a spurious SequenceGap error on startup.

llrs.title: Sequence counter gap updates last_seq even when rejecting the frame
llrs.description: When a sequence gap is detected, `Receiver::unwrap()` shall update `last_seq` to the received counter before returning the error, so that subsequent frames are evaluated against the latest observed counter.
llrs.rationale: Prevents a single dropped frame from causing all subsequent frames to also be rejected.
llrs.implementation: src/safety/mod.rs — `Receiver::unwrap()` gap block: `state.last_seq = seq` before returning Err

llrs.title: HeaderTooShort check occurs before any slice indexing
llrs.description: The length guard in `Receiver::unwrap()` shall be the first operation before any byte access into the data slice, to prevent out-of-bounds panics.
llrs.rationale: A panic in an ASIL-B function would constitute an uncontrolled failure; the guard must be unconditional.
